title: Cynanchum utahense
intro: Cynanchum utahense, also known as Utah milkweed or Utah swallowwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asclepiadaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer activities. The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum utahense include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its potential medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Cynanchum utahense include teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
taxonomy: Cynanchum utahense is classified within the plant family Apocynaceae. This family is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The Apocynaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Cynanchum utahense belonging to the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. Asclepiadoideae is a subfamily of flowering plants that contains around 2,000 species. Cynanchum utahense is a species of milkvine native to North America.
